name: Zez Confrey
abstract: Edward Elzear "Zez" Confrey (3 April 1895 – 22 November 1971) was an American composer and performer of novelty piano and jazz music. His most noted works were "Kitten on the Keys" and "Dizzy Fingers." Studying at the Chicago Musical College and becoming enthralled by French impressionists played a critical role in how he composed and pe…

about_quotes.speaker: Edwin "Squirrel" Ashcraft
about_quotes.evidence: I think the first piano that impressed me at all was the all-star trio, Oman and Arden, later on Zez Confrey.

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Hyman
about_quotes.evidence: even stuff by Zez Confrey and ragtime people like that, novelty piano people like that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Hyman interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]
